title: Homer To The Max

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's typical mishap
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer D'oh reaction
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment but routine for the show - perfectly executed but expected

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Door? Locked. Phone? Unplugged. Dog? Cat? Taped and corked.
jokes.setup: Family preparing for mid-season TV premiere with systematic preparation
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable (door, phone) to absurd animal abuse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect economy - absurdist peak lands well

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Alright family, we need to prepare for the mid-season premiere. First, we lock the door so no one can bother us.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's very thoughtful, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Second, we unplug the phone.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Good thinking, Dad.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Third, we tape a sign to the window that says 'Do Not Disturb.'

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: What about the dog?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We chain Santa's Little Helper to the radiator.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And we beat the cat with a newspaper.

jokes.text: All in the Family 1999 - They got me livin' with an African-American, a Semite-American and a woman-American there! And I'm glad! I love youse all!
jokes.setup: Parody of classic sitcom updated for late 90s PC culture
jokes.punchline: Archie Bunker forced into politically correct language while maintaining his voice
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural satire that captures both the original character and 90s sensibilities perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Archie Bunker Parody
dialogue.line: Now look here, you... uh... vertically challenged individual of unspecified ethnic origin. Back in my day, we could say what we meant without some college-educated meathead telling us we're offensive.

dialogue.speaker: Archie Bunker Parody
dialogue.line: But nowadays I gotta watch every word. Can't call 'em what they are without some sensitivity coordinator breathing down my neck. So I'm supposed to say 'follicly challenged' instead of bald? That's not progress, that's just lying with extra steps!

dialogue.speaker: Archie Bunker Parody
dialogue.line: The worst part? I still sound like the same old Archie, just with a thesaurus up my... well, I can't say that anymore either.

jokes.text: Networks like animation 'cause they don't have to pay the actors squat! Plus they can replace them, and no one can tell the diddly-ifference.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why networks like animation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on The Simpsons itself, with Ned Flanders voice impression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary that works on multiple levels - the show commenting on its own industry

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Networks love animation because they can use the same jokes over and over again, and nobody complains.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Okily-dokily, that's not very neighborly! *in Ned Flanders voice*

jokes.text: It's hard to believe someone that young could have risen to the rank of admiral. Gee! I never thought I'd say this about a TV show, but this is kind of stupid.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on the absurdity of Admiral Baby
jokes.punchline: Lisa, the voice of reason, calling out bad TV while being on TV herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - her intelligence and moral clarity used for meta-commentary

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: This is the worst thing I've ever seen on television.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But you're watching it.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I know! That's what makes it so ironic!

jokes.text: Let's get this bank back to our hideout, and we'll break into it later.
jokes.setup: Criminals planning a bank robbery
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical plan to steal the entire bank building
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with good structure - the logic is perfectly illogical

dialogue.speaker: TV Criminal
dialogue.line: Alright men, here's the plan. We'll use a helicopter to lift the entire bank building and fly it to Mexico.

dialogue.speaker: TV Criminal
dialogue.line: Nobody will ever suspect that we stole the whole building.

jokes.text: It's the cops! Worse. The police cops.
jokes.setup: Criminals react to sirens
jokes.punchline: Police Cops are somehow worse than regular cops
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective escalation joke - the redundancy is the point

dialogue.speaker: Criminal 1
dialogue.line: Oh no, the cops are coming!

dialogue.speaker: Criminal 2
dialogue.line: Wait, those aren't regular cops...

dialogue.speaker: Criminal 1
dialogue.line: Those are Police Cops!

dialogue.speaker: Criminal 2
dialogue.line: We're doomed. They're somehow worse than regular cops.

jokes.text: Tell me one thing mankind's ever done that's any better. The Renaissance. This is better.
jokes.setup: Homer claims nothing is better than Police Cops
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses the Renaissance in favor of a bad cop show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - the confidence and specificity make this exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Nothing is better than Police Cops.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, what about the Renaissance?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Eh, Police Cops is on.

jokes.text: And that's the end of that chapter.
jokes.setup: TV Homer's catchphrase after solving cases
jokes.punchline: Overly literary catchphrase for a simple cop
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag effectively but not particularly funny on its own

dialogue.speaker: TV Homer
dialogue.line: Case closed. Elementary, my dear Watson. The perpetrator's modus operandi was transparently pellucid, their felonious machinations rendered asunder by the inexorable march of deductive reasoning. Verily, I have once again demonstrated my unparalleled acumen in the investigative arts. Egad.

jokes.text: His name's like my name!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes the TV character shares his name
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike excitement over obvious coincidence
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment that establishes the premise - functional but not inspired

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... that guy's name is Homer Simpson too!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! That's me!

jokes.text: Well, let's just say I... hate crime.
jokes.setup: TV Homer asked why he doesn't live it up as a millionaire
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous statement that could mean fighting crime or hating crime
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - good writing for the fake show

dialogue.speaker: TV Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not a millionaire. I'm just a guy who fights crime.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Character
dialogue.line: You mean you hate crime?

dialogue.speaker: TV Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's what I said.

jokes.text: Did you see the way Daddy caught that bullet? That's not really you, Dad. Don't confuse Daddy, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Bart thinks Homer actually caught a bullet
jokes.punchline: Marge protects Homer's delusion rather than supporting reality
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic joke showing how they enable Homer's fantasies

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad actually caught a bullet with his bare hands!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: No he didn't, Bart. He just picked it up off the ground.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Lisa! Why would you say that? Homer caught that bullet. I saw it with my own eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Mom, you were in the other room.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, I'm choosing to believe Homer's version of events.

jokes.text: like that guy named Anthony Michael Hall who stole your car stereo.
jokes.setup: Marge gives example of coincidental names
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity reference for petty crime
jokes.explanation: Weird specific reference that's funny for its randomness and unexpectedness

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, like when someone's name is so perfectly suited to what they do. For example, there's a jewel thief named Sticky Fingers McGee.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, that's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: And a bank robber named Jimmy Two-Times.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What about that guy who keeps stealing copper wire from the power plant?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh yes, Sparky the Kleptomaniac.

jokes.text: Y'ello. Yes, this is the original Homer Simpson.
jokes.setup: Homer answers phone expecting fame
jokes.punchline: Homer claims to be the 'original' when he's clearly not
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's ego and delusion well but predictable development

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hello? Is this about my Grammy?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer, it's Moe. Listen, I'm calling about that song you recorded.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, 'Baby On Board'? I'm the original, baby!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer, that song came out in 1990. You recorded yours last week.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I'm the *original* original!

jokes.text: The Debbie Pinson, who was the homecoming queen in high school? Yes, I'm still available. No, he's not!
jokes.setup: Homer thinks attractive woman is calling him
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately shuts down his fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic comedy - Homer's fantasy vs reality

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hello?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, it's me.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh... hi Marge.

jokes.text: Homer, we've worked together for 10 years. It's Carl. You only wrote my name. Um, I wanted yours. Take it or leave it, 'Carl.'
jokes.setup: Carl asks Homer for his autograph
jokes.punchline: Homer gives Carl his own name back as an autograph
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely misunderstands what an autograph is but acts with authority

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, can I get your autograph?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sure, Carl.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Thanks! Wait... you just signed my name.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Take it or leave it, 'Carl.'
jokes.setup: Carl complains about the wrong autograph
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive response with air quotes around Carl's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up - Homer's condescending tone makes the moment even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Homer, you signed this autograph to the wrong person. It says 'To Lenny.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, well, maybe I was thinking of "Carl" that day.

jokes.text: So they really based that Homer Simpson character on you, huh? Yup. Right down to the scarf.
jokes.setup: Moe asks if TV character is based on Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer claims credit for details that aren't even his
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's delusion - taking credit for things that aren't even accurate

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, is that character on TV based on you?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You bet it is! I came up with the whole thing - the stupid laugh, the beer belly, the way he says 'D'oh!'

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer, that character is a space alien.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! I invented that too!

jokes.text: I was supervising the Guns for Toys program. It's Toys for Guns! Now you tell me.
jokes.setup: TV Homer explains his mistake
jokes.punchline: He completely reversed the premise of the program
jokes.explanation: Excellent stupidity joke - the reversal makes perfect sense for a stupid character

dialogue.speaker: TV Homer
dialogue.line: He completely reversed the premise of the program

jokes.text: Hey, what's goin' on? That guy's not Homer Simpson. He's fat and stupid.
jokes.setup: Barney reacts to TV Homer being portrayed as stupid
jokes.punchline: Barney calls Homer fat and stupid while defending him
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Barney accidentally insults Homer while trying to defend him

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Hey, Homer's not stupid! He's fat and stupid.

jokes.text: Maybe he's just acting stupid to infiltrate a gang of international idiots. Yeah, th-that's gotta be it.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to rationalize TV character's stupidity
jokes.punchline: His explanation is equally stupid - international idiot gang
jokes.explanation: 'International idiots' is wonderfully absurd and shows Homer's defensive rationalization

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, you see, that character isn't stupid. He's part of an international idiot gang. They have meetings and everything. Secret handshake and all that.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm thinking about joining myself. The initiation is you have to do something really dumb in front of everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've already got my outfit picked out.

jokes.text: That was my insulin! Uh-oh, SpaghettiOs!
jokes.setup: TV Homer destroyed what he thought was drugs
jokes.punchline: It was life-saving medicine, responds with childish catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Dark humor of potentially deadly mistake followed by inappropriately cheerful response

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Shut up! I'm not your clown! Don't diminish me!
jokes.setup: Homer is asked to act stupid like his TV character
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic outburst about his dignity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's wounded pride and self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not just an actor playing a stupid guy. I have dignity! I have self-respect!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: [Homer falls through floor] Geez. What an exit. Oh, man. What's he gonna do for an encore?
jokes.setup: Homer storms out dramatically
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermined by falling through floor, friends comment like it's performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing - dramatic moment immediately punctured by slapstick

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's it! I'm leaving this bar forever!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *crashes through floor*

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Well, that was quite an exit.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Yeah, really drove home the point.

jokes.text: like, oh, Marlon Brando in Apocalypse Now.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains Homer provides comic relief
jokes.punchline: Compares Homer to Brando in a dark, serious war film
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected reference - Lisa's intelligence creates sophisticated comparison

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad provides comic relief. He's like Marlon Brando in Apocalypse Now, except instead of 'I love the smell of napalm in the morning,' it's 'I love the smell of donuts in the morning.'

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry to disappoint you gentlemen, but you seem to have me confused with a character in a fictional show. Now, if you'll excuse me, my fondue is just about- D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer tries to act sophisticated and distinguished
jokes.punchline: Immediately does exactly what his TV character would do
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - Homer's attempt at dignity immediately undermined by classic behavior

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm going to act sophisticated and distinguished.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: D'oh! - There goes Albany! - Uh-oh, SpaghettiOs!
jokes.setup: Homer drops his fondue
jokes.punchline: People treat it like the TV show with catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing how life imitates the show, though not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh no, my fondue!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Don't worry Homer, people treat it like the TV show with catchphrases.

jokes.text: Let's get out of here. Forget the baby's medicine! But her forehead's on fire! Fine. I'll be in the car, driving home.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to leave due to embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes his ego over sick baby, then gives contradictory instruction
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Homer's selfishness plus logical contradiction of driving home while leaving

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm leaving! This is too embarrassing!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, Maggie is sick!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't care! My ego is more important!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, don't let her die while I'm gone!

jokes.text: Worst... reading... ever.
jokes.setup: Homer poorly delivers his TV catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy uses his own catchphrase to critique
jokes.explanation: Good character moment using established catchphrase appropriately

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Worst. Catchphrase. Ever.

jokes.text: That reminds me, do you mind if my nephew kicks you in the belly? Okay. [Grunt] Oh! Aw, the flash didn't go off. You got another one in you, Josh?
jokes.setup: Fan wants photo with Homer
jokes.punchline: Escalates to physical abuse, then demands repeat when photo fails
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fan absurdity and Homer's passive acceptance of abuse

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: Can I get a photo with you?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sure!

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: Wait, it didn't come out. Take another one!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! Ow!

jokes.text: Bart, kill that cat! And that big yellow flower! Homer, your growing insanity is starting to worry me.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks everything is mocking him
jokes.punchline: Paranoia extends to harmless objects, Lisa's understated reaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of paranoia with Lisa's perfectly understated psychiatric assessment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why is that cloud laughing at me?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, it's just a cloud.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh sure, that's what YOU'D say if you were in on it!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: In on what, man?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The conspiracy! Look at that tree — it's mocking me with its branches!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, trees don't mock people.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Not with THAT attitude!

jokes.text: Yeah. 'Bart.'
jokes.setup: Homer told to watch 'that weird-looking kid'
jokes.punchline: Homer suspicious of his own son
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective paranoia joke - Homer's suspicion of his own family

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That weird-looking kid? Wait a minute... that's my son!

jokes.text: The 13 of us began with a singular vision- Titanic meets Frasier. But then we found out that ABC had a similar project in development with Annie Potts and Jeremy Piven. Who's Jeremy Piven? We don't know.
jokes.setup: Producer explains the show's development
jokes.punchline: Afraid of unknown actor, absurd high-concept mashup
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Hollywood development process and fear of competition from unknowns

dialogue.speaker: Producer
dialogue.line: We were afraid to cast an unknown actor in the lead role, so we decided to combine three established franchises into one show to guarantee success.

dialogue.speaker: Producer
dialogue.line: It's basically 'Game of Thrones' meets 'The Bachelor' meets 'Antiques Roadshow.'

jokes.text: Who's Jeremy Piven? - We don't know. But it scared the hell out of us, so we slapped together a cop show instead.
jokes.setup: Producers mention fear of competition
jokes.punchline: They don't know who their competition is but panicked anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of Hollywood development panic - the absurd logic is perfect

dialogue.speaker: TV Producer
dialogue.line: We're facing some serious competition.

dialogue.speaker: TV Producer
dialogue.line: We don't even know who it is.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, you should panic anyway!

jokes.text: The network idiots didn't want a show about high-tech badges that shoot laser beams.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Badge Patrol was changed
jokes.punchline: Reveals the original concept was completely ridiculous sci-fi
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept that satirizes bad high-concept TV ideas

dialogue.speaker: Producer
dialogue.line: Well, you see, the original concept for Badge Patrol was that the officers would fight crime on Mars using telepathic dolphins.

jokes.text: Oh, he's not stupid. He's a street-smart fish out of water in a world he never made. I'm begging you! I'm a human being! Let me have my dignity back!
jokes.setup: Producer defends character while Homer pleads
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposition of producer's BS with Homer's genuine pain
jokes.explanation: Strong contrast between Hollywood spin and human emotion creates effective comedy

dialogue.speaker: Producer
dialogue.line: Look, we stand by our characters. They're complex, nuanced individuals.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: My son's been missing for three days!

dialogue.speaker: Producer
dialogue.line: It's called dramatic tension. The audience loves it.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: He's eight years old! Please, just tell me where Bart is!

jokes.text: Now he's stupid and whiny.
jokes.setup: TV Homer begs for dignity
jokes.punchline: Bart notes the character has gotten worse
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke - the character degradation continues

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Please, I'm begging you. Have some dignity.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dude, you've gotten way worse than that.

jokes.text: Hercules Rockefeller. Rembrandt Q. Einstein. Handsome B. Wonderful.
jokes.setup: Homer's name change options
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous fake-sounding names
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - each name more ridiculous than the last, highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I could change my name to something cool like... Rooster.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Or Maverick.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Or Thrillho!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Or... Max Power!

jokes.text: I'm going to give you the only name you spelled correctly. From this day forward, your name shall be- 'Max Power'?
jokes.setup: Judge will give Homer the name he spelled correctly
jokes.punchline: Of all the elaborate names, he only spelled the simplest one correctly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - can't spell elaborate names but gets the action-hero one right

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, of all the elaborate names presented, you managed to spell only one correctly.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Homer.

jokes.text: That's the way Max Power is, Marge- decisive, uncompromising and rude.
jokes.setup: Homer explains his new personality
jokes.punchline: Lists rudeness as a positive trait
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Homer thinks negative traits are positive when rebranded

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've adopted a new personality. I'm rude, I'm crude, I'm socially unacceptable.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And you know what? It's working out great!

jokes.text: And he traded a mule for it. And that mule went on to save spring break.
jokes.setup: Grampa explains family name history
jokes.punchline: Random claim about mule's heroic spring break rescue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa moment - starts with plausible family history, veers into nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Back in my day, the Simpson name meant something. My great-grandfather once saved an entire town from a wildfire by riding his mule through the streets warning everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Dad, that's not even close to what happened.

dialogue.speaker: Grampa Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, actually it was a mule that saved me during spring break in Reno. Heroic animal. Probably in a museum somewhere now.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, they have acids that can burn that off.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about tattoo of Homer's name
jokes.punchline: Homer casually suggests acid treatment for intimate tattoo
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Homer's casual attitude toward painful solution

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I'm worried about that tattoo of my name on your... well, you know.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, that? Don't worry, Marge. I can just use some acid to get rid of it.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Acid?! Homer, that's dangerous!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Nah, I'll be fine. It's just a little acid.

jokes.text: Nobody snuggles with Max Power. You strap yourself in and feel the G's!
jokes.setup: Marge doesn't want to snuggle with Max Power
jokes.punchline: Homer describes cuddling in extreme sports terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - reframing intimacy as extreme action. Highly quotable and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Cuddling is like extreme sports. You gotta commit 100%, throw your body into it, and hope you don't break anything.

jokes.text: Kids, there's three ways to do things- the right way, the wrong way and the Max Power way. - Isn't that the wrong way? - Yeah, but faster!
jokes.setup: Homer presenting three ways to do things
jokes.punchline: Admitting Max Power way is wrong but faster
jokes.explanation: Near-perfect setup/punchline structure with excellent character consistency. Highly quotable and became a cultural reference.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: There's three ways to do things: the right way, the wrong way, and the Max Power way.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Isn't that the wrong way?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, but faster!

jokes.text: Kids, there's three ways to do things- the right way, the wrong way and the Max Power way. Isn't that the wrong way? Yeah, but faster!
jokes.setup: Homer explains three ways to do things
jokes.punchline: Max Power way is just the wrong way but faster
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with Lisa's logic destroying Homer's philosophy perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: There's three ways to do things: the right way, the wrong way, and the Max Power way.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Isn't that just the wrong way?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, but faster.

jokes.text: Well, who could forget the name of a magnetic individual like you? Keep up the good work, Max. Mr. Power. Yes, of course. Mr. Power.
jokes.setup: Burns claims Homer is unforgettable
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets his name wrong, has to be corrected
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment - pompous claims immediately undermined by failure

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're unforgettable, Simpson.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, Sampson. I'll never forget you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's Simpson.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, yes, that's what I said.

jokes.text: Max Power doesn't abbreviate. Each letter is as important as the one that preceded it. Maybe more important. No, as important.
jokes.setup: Homer insists on full name on monogram
jokes.punchline: Philosophical rambling about letter importance with self-correction
jokes.explanation: Good Max Power philosophy - overthinking simple things with fake profundity

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I want my monogram to have my full name: H-J-S!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But Homer, that's three letters. A monogram is usually just initials.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't care! Every letter is important. H is for Homer, J is for Jay, and S is for Simpson. Without the J, I'm just H-S, which sounds like I'm hissing at people!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Actually, wait... I'm already Simpson. So that's H-Simpson. And without the J, I'm just... Homer Simpson. Which is fine, actually. Never mind.

jokes.text: If you don't, who will, huh? Trent Steel. Homer Simp- Uh, Max Power. Ooh, hey! Great name! Yeah, isn't it? I got it off a hair dryer.
jokes.setup: Homer meets sophisticated Trent Steel
jokes.punchline: Admits his cool name came from appliance
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Homer's pretensions - reveals mundane source of 'powerful' name

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, Trent Steel? That's the coolest name I've ever heard!

dialogue.speaker: Trent Steel
dialogue.line: Thanks. It's actually from a dryer and a can of beans.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Trent Steel
dialogue.line: My dad combined 'Trent' from a Kenmore dryer and 'Steel' from a Brawny paper towel can.

jokes.text: You like Thai? Tie good. You like shirt?
jokes.setup: Trent asks about Thai food
jokes.punchline: Homer mishears and responds about neckties and clothing
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay joke showing Homer's lack of sophistication

dialogue.speaker: Trent
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, you ever been to that Thai place down the street?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Thai? Oh yeah, I got a bunch of ties at home. Some with little ducks on 'em.

jokes.text: He has a company that makes computers, or a computer that makes companies. You wouldn't understand.
jokes.setup: Homer explains Trent's business
jokes.punchline: Completely confused explanation ending with dismissive superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - admits confusion then acts superior. Great character writing

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, he's got this thing where he takes the money from one guy and gives it to another guy, and then... uh... I think there's a guy in the middle somewhere who gets a cut. It's called... uh... 'business.' Yeah, that's it. I don't really understand it, but I know I'm smarter than him.

jokes.text: The house number is spelled out with letters. Get used to it, honey. From now on we'll be spelling everything with letters.
jokes.setup: Homer notices fancy house details
jokes.punchline: Plans to spell everything with letters as if this is revolutionary
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stupidity - doesn't understand that letters are how spelling works

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, look at this fancy house! You know what this place needs? I'm going to spell everything with letters. Like, instead of saying 'couch', I'll say 'C-O-U-C-H'. Revolutionary!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's just... spelling.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! Why didn't anyone think of this before?

jokes.text: I can't believe those pants are made entirely out of hemp. And this one solar collector can gather enough energy to run this colorful pinwheel.
jokes.setup: Celebrity environmental enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Begley's solar innovation only powers a toy pinwheel
jokes.explanation: Good satire of environmental showboating - impressive tech for trivial result

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: I've installed solar panels on my entire house!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow, that's great, Ed. What do they power?

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: Well, this toy pinwheel.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's it?

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: Well, and it's completely silent.

jokes.text: I feel like Cinderella. Me too. Let's sing the Cinderella song. Eh, maybe later.
jokes.setup: Marge excited about fancy party
jokes.punchline: Homer deflates musical moment with casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Homer's lack of romantic enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, this party is going to be absolutely wonderful! The champagne, the orchestra, the elegant guests...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah. Is there gonna be food?

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Here comes Lorne Michaels. Pretend you don't see him.
jokes.setup: SNL producer Lorne Michaels approaches
jokes.punchline: Even at a Hollywood party, people avoid him
jokes.explanation: Good industry inside joke - plays off Michaels' reputation in comedy circles

dialogue.speaker: Party Guest
dialogue.line: Is that Lorne Michaels over there?

dialogue.speaker: Party Guest
dialogue.line: Yeah, let's go talk to someone else.

jokes.text: So I said, 'If this is the House of Pancakes, how come I can't eat the walls?'
jokes.setup: Homer telling a story at the party
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of the restaurant name
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Homer's literal thinking appealing to sophisticated audience.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So I went to this restaurant called 'The Krusty Krab'...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And I spent the whole time waiting for an actual crab to come out and fight me!

jokes.text: So I said, 'If this is the House of Pancakes, how come I can't eat the walls?' Oh, you are too much, Max.
jokes.setup: Homer tells anecdote at sophisticated party
jokes.punchline: Stupid literal interpretation of restaurant name gets positive reaction
jokes.explanation: Shows how Max Power persona makes Homer's stupidity seem charming

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So I went to this new restaurant called 'The Elbow Room.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I thought it meant you could relax and stretch out, you know? So I took off my shirt and put my elbows on the table.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Turns out it's just a regular restaurant. But the manager loved it! He said I was a natural!

dialogue.speaker: Party Guest
dialogue.line: Ha! That's hilarious!

jokes.text: Hell, I've done it with pigs. Real no-foolin' pigs.
jokes.setup: Clinton being overly familiar with Marge
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of bestiality
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Clinton's scandals taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Quebec's got the bomb. Well, I gotta go, but, look, if you're ever near the White House, there's a toolshed out back. I'm in there most of the day.
jokes.setup: Clinton gets urgent news
jokes.punchline: President of the US spends time in toolshed, gives directions for affair
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on presidential behavior and priorities during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Clinton
dialogue.line: I need to get to the toolshed immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Aide
dialogue.line: Mr. President, what's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Clinton
dialogue.line: Never mind that. Just tell the press I'm working on... infrastructure.

dialogue.speaker: Clinton
dialogue.line: And if anyone asks, I was giving detailed directions to the shed. Very detailed directions. To a young intern.

jokes.text: Hey, no problem!
jokes.setup: Host talking about being blessed with privilege
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of serious social responsibility talk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's inability to read social situations and take things seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: We're blessed with privilege and it comes with responsibility to help those less fortunate.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, responsibility... Mmm... donuts.

jokes.text: Give something back? Boo! Get a haircut! Somebody buy this guy a tree so he'll shut up!
jokes.setup: Environmental speech about giving back
jokes.punchline: Homer's aggressive anti-environmental heckling
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's oblivious selfishness contrasted with progressive party guests

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's great, but where's the free beer?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not listening to any more of this tree-hugging nonsense! Trees are just taking up space!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And don't even get me started on those spotted owls. More like spotted pests!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The only thing I want to give back is my trash!

jokes.text: It's a go-cart powered by my own sense of self-satisfaction.
jokes.setup: Begley explains his alternative transportation
jokes.punchline: Vehicle powered by smugness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of environmental virtue signaling - perfectly captures the character type

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: This vehicle is powered entirely by smugness.

jokes.text: Remember that New Year's Eve at Lenny's? He didn't even have a clock.
jokes.setup: Marge comparing bad parties
jokes.punchline: Lenny's New Year's party lacked basic New Year's equipment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective absurdity - missing the one essential item for New Year's

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, Lenny's New Year's party was even worse. He didn't have a single ball to drop.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: I was gonna use one of my balls, but I couldn't find it.

jokes.text: 'Swab'? I thought it was a spray. Okay. She's second.
jokes.setup: Chief orders Eddie to swab eyes with Mace
jokes.punchline: Eddie confused about technique, Chief casually assigns Marge as second victim
jokes.explanation: Good police incompetence comedy with casual escalation of violence

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Eddie, I want you to swab his eyes with mace.

dialogue.speaker: Eddie
dialogue.line: Swab his eyes, Chief? I don't understand the technique.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Just do it, Eddie. And when you're done, do Marge.

jokes.text: Go swab Begley. He likes it.
jokes.setup: Chief directing police violence
jokes.punchline: Suggests Begley enjoys being maced
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about police brutality and environmental activist stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: He's just a little excited. Hey Begley, you want another blast of the mace?

dialogue.speaker: Officer Eddie
dialogue.line: Yeah, Chief, he's been asking for it!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, if you insist... *sprays mace* There ya go, Begley. You know, I think he actually enjoys this.

jokes.text: That car was just two days away from retirement.
jokes.setup: Police car crashes and explodes
jokes.punchline: Classic cop movie trope about partner/equipment near retirement
jokes.explanation: Good parody of overused action movie cliché applied to inanimate object

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Oh no! My car! She was three days away from retirement!

dialogue.speaker: Officer Eddie
dialogue.line: Chief, what about me? I'm two days away from retirement!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Sorry Eddie, the car's more important.

jokes.text: While I was at the courthouse, I had them change your name. To what? Chesty LaRue.
jokes.setup: Homer reveals he changed Marge's name too
jokes.punchline: Chose ridiculous stripper-like name for his wife
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Homer's selfishness and poor judgment in name selection

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I changed your name to Chesty LaRue.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: You did WHAT?!

jokes.text: Fine. Hooty McBoob it is.
jokes.setup: Marge rejects both names
jokes.punchline: Homer's final, most ridiculous option
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the escalation - Hooty McBoob is memorably absurd

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What about... Pukahontas?

jokes.text: If you're not completely satisfied, you can be Busty St. Clair. Fine. Hooty McBoob it is.
jokes.setup: Homer offers alternative names
jokes.punchline: Each option worse than the last, ending with ridiculous breast reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with increasingly absurd names, highly quotable ending

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We could name him Bart.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's your name.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We could name him Bort.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's not a real name.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We could name him Bobo.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's a monkey's name.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We could name him Melons. Just two big beautiful melons.

tmdb_overview: A new TV series features a suave and debonair character named Homer Simpson. Homer capitalizes on his new namesake, until the character is rewritten to be a bumbling fool. Trying to recapture the positive attention he enjoyed, Homer changes his name to "Max Power."
insight: Homer's identity crisis sparks 68 jokes at 2.92-per-minute clip for Season 10 peak.
